item.title: Four people die trying to board boat in Channel crossing attempt
item.description: Rescue efforts remain under way after the incident off the coast of northern France, local media reports.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Israeli Strikes in Lebanon Threaten Shaky U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire
item.description: Lebanon was in mourning after Israeli forces killed at least 182 people in strikes against Hezbollah, the Iran-backed militant group, on Wednesday. Disagreements have surfaced over whether the cease-fire applies to Lebanon.

item.title: The Hit Erotica Writers Outwitting Nigeria’s Religious Censors
item.description: Zealous officials burned their predecessors’ romance novels. Now, young Muslim women in northern Nigeria publish their erotic books in installments on WhatsApp.

item.title: He's Australia's most decorated soldier. Now he's at the centre of a historic war crimes case
item.description: Ben Roberts-Smith's case is not only unprecedented for Australia but "extraordinary" for the globe too, scholars say.

item.title: Cooper urges full and toll-free reopening of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The foreign secretary also calls for Lebanon to be "urgently included" in the ceasefire agreement.

item.title: Lebanon Mourns After Israeli Barrage Kills at Least 182 People
item.description: The intense bombardment of more than 100 airstrikes in 10 minutes was a sharp escalation that put pressure on the shaky cease-fire with Iran.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
OECD urges governments to rapidly unwind costly fuel duty cuts
item.description: More than 25 countries have introduced universal measures to shield consumers from high energy prices since the start of the Iran war

item.title: Key Indian state polls begin in test for Modi's party
item.description: Modi's Bharatiya Janata Party is trying to expand into regions where it has struggled to gain a foothold.

item.title: BDO axes 31 partner roles as AI pressure grows and profits fall
item.description: Move comes amid downturn for professional services after hiring spree during pandemic

item.title: The ceasefire bounce
item.description: And the Hormuz bitcoin tollbooth

item.title: Plaid Cymru promises action on NHS, childcare and economy
item.description: Plaid leader says Plaid government to cut waiting times, help with childcare costs and grow economy.

item.title: Europe nervously eyes fragile Iran ceasefire as energy crisis rumbles on
item.description: Also in this newsletter: Italy calls for looser EU spending rules

item.title: FirstFT: US-Iran truce shows signs of strain
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: private credit redemption requests and EU faces ‘stagflationary shock’

item.title: Tariffs are Trump’s perpetual leverage machine
item.description: And the US might find them hard to give up

item.title: Man Is Arrested in Bahamas Days After Michigan Woman Goes Missing
item.description: The Bahamian police said they had arrested an American man in connection with the disappearance of Lynette Hooker, who fell off a dinghy while boating with her husband.

item.title: A Cease-Fire for Now in Iran, but a Blow to American Credibility
item.description: Critics wonder if this is America’s “Suez moment,” when a leading power signals the start of its international decline.

item.title: He Got Rich Buying and Selling Luxury Watches. Was It a Ponzi Scheme?
item.description: Dominic Khoo made waves in Singapore as an investor in expensive timepieces. Now many of his clients accuse him of fraud.

item.title: No, Britain Is Not Having a Christian Revival
item.description: A study said church attendance had soared among British young people, a trend reversal that excited religious conservatives around the world. Turns out it wasn’t true.

item.title: Investors sought to pull $20bn from private credit funds in first quarter
item.description: Big groups including Apollo, Ares and Blackstone were hit with redemption requests at start of 2026

item.title: Shipping stalls as Tehran dictates terms in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Despite US-Iran ceasefire deal fewer tankers passed the crucial waterway than during fiercest days of fighting

item.title: Ackman in talks to launch fund to bet on investor complacency
item.description: Strategy would echo doomsday trades that earned Pershing Square a big windfall during the pandemic

item.title: UK becomes top destination for US jet fuel after Gulf flow squeeze
item.description: Iran war stokes embrace of American exports as Britain’s aviation sector shifts reliance on Middle East supplies

item.title: US oil exports to hit record as Iran war triggers race for supplies
item.description: ‘Armada’ of Asian tankers heads for American ports as energy shortages spread

item.title: EU will still be hit by ‘stagflationary shock’, Brussels warns
item.description: European Commission preparing to cut growth forecasts despite two-week Iran war ceasefire

item.title: CLSA curtails flagship Tokyo forum because of its Chinese parent
item.description: Famed investor gathering becomes ‘access day’ with new event launched in South Korea

item.title: Iran war has exposed the weakness of the dollar
item.description: As Opec members have long understood, it is not a good idea to give users of your product an incentive to find alternatives

item.title: Is the BoJ ready to take Japan back to reality?
item.description: This month’s probable rate increase is in keeping with the moment

item.title: The case for trade, remade
item.description: There are good reasons to believe it is likely to follow the rewiring of global finance earlier this century

item.title: Have Hungarians had enough of Orbán?
item.description: Rural strongholds turn against ruling Fidesz party ahead of Sunday’s parliamentary election

item.title: Switzerland’s referendum habit: an early warning to Europe?
item.description: As the country considers capping its population, the drawbacks of direct democracy are up for debate

item.title: Reform UK donor runs company linked to sanctioned Iranian conglomerate
item.description: Dubai-based Orico described by French court as ‘subsidiary’ of a group that is majority owned by foundation with close ties to Islamic regime

item.title: ‘It’s not at all the palazzo I imagined’: Valeria Napoleone moves to Milan
item.description: The art and design collector has turned a bronze glass penthouse into a singular home where Memphis meets madcap

item.title: Is BTS losing its K-pop identity as it aims for a bigger world stage?
item.description: BTS turned millions on to K-pop. But now it’s caught between Korea and the world.

item.title: Disagreement Over Lebanon’s Inclusion in Cease-Fire Threatens to Unravel It
item.description: The U.S. says the deal didn’t include the country, but Iran says it did. Israel is bombarding Lebanon, and Iran wants to show it supports its allies.

item.title: Chinese researcher's death after questioning in US prompts anger in Beijing
item.description: The semiconductor researcher was found dead after “hostile questioning” by US law enforcement.

item.title: US man arrested in case of Michigan woman's disappearance in Bahamas
item.description: Lynette Hooker fell overboard from a small boat and was swept out to sea by strong currents, her husband told authorities.

item.title: Trump Finds Himself With Fragile Iran Cease-Fire After the Scramble to Make a Deal
item.description: After careening from one diplomatic extreme to another, President Trump finds himself with a fragile deal that is already showing signs of fraying.

item.title: Starmer says much work remains to make US-Iran ceasefire hold
item.description: The prime minister says fully reopening the Strait of Hormuz would help "stabilise" prices in the UK.

item.title: NHS questions dominate election leaders' programme
item.description: The Conservatives, Plaid Cymru and the Liberal Democrats faced audience questions in Haverfordwest.

item.title: Israel backs US-Iran ceasefire but Netanyahu's war goals remain unfulfilled
item.description: The Israeli prime minister's response to the ceasefire has been muted, in contrast to the triumphal statements from the US and Iran.

item.title: At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes
item.description: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.

item.title: Negotiators face huge task to close gaps in rival Iran peace proposals
item.description: A US 15-point plan and an Iranian 10-point variant are oceans apart, writes the BBC's diplomatic correspondent.

item.title: Canada’s Mark Carney Poised to Secure Liberal Party Majority After Gladu’s Defection
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney’s Liberal Party is expected to win two special elections that could give the government broader powers in Parliament.

item.title: Oil prices plunge and shares jump on US-Iran ceasefire plan
item.description: Crude prices tumbled by as much as 15% on the conditional pause but remain far higher than before the war.

item.title: Even as They Praise Iran Cease-Fire, World Leaders Are Whipsawed by Trump
item.description: Across Europe and the globe, the war has damaged economies, roiled politics and underscored a lack of options in dealing with President Trump’s whims.

item.title: Gambia appoints British barrister to prosecute gruesome Jammeh-era crimes
item.description: Ex-President Yahya Jammeh's 22-year rule was known for enforced disappearances and extrajudicial killings.

item.title: China Pressed Iran Toward Cease-Fire, Iranian Officials Say
item.description: Beijing appeared to have helped push Tehran to accept the two-week deal with the United States, reflecting China’s growing influence and its stake in avoiding a wider war.

item.title: Greece Plans to Block Social Media for Children Under 15
item.description: Countries including Australia, Spain and others have already passed bans or are working on plans to restrict teenagers’ access to social sites.

item.title: Who is Viktor Orbán, Hungarian PM fighting to stay in power after 16 years?
item.description: Since 2010, Orbán has transformed Hungary into what the European Parliament has denounced as a "hybrid regime of electoral autocracy".

item.title: Gas Prices Won’t Quickly Return to Prewar Levels, Even if the Strait of Hormuz Reopens
item.description: Some wells can be turned on in days or weeks, but bringing the Gulf’s energy system back to something akin to normal will take months.

item.title: Partner, scapegoat or spoiler? Israel’s place in a fragile ceasefire
item.description: The dealmaking between America and Iran is happening without Binyamin Netanyahu

item.title: Iran Strait of Hormuz warning adds to shipping uncertainty
item.description: Only a few vessels have crossed the strait since the US-Iran ceasefire deal, according to BBC Verify analysis.

item.title: Why ceasefire deal with US has unsettled Iran's hardliners
item.description: The two-week truce opens up the prospect of direct talks with the US but has angered Iran's hardliners, writes BBC News Persian’s Kasra Naji.

item.title: Watch: Fire damages roof of Rio's Olympic velodrome
item.description: Around 80 firefighters and 20 fire trucks tackled the blaze, the state's military fire department said, adding that no one was hurt.

item.title: Bowen: Ceasefire means respite for civilians, but it might not last long
item.description: Whether or not a lasting peace deal can be reached, the war and its consequences are reshaping the Middle East, writes international editor Jeremy Bowen.

item.title: Government approves UK's largest solar farm
item.description: The Springwell solar farm in North Kesteven would cover an area the size of 1,700 football pitches.

item.title: How France learned to fight Russian disinformation
item.description: The EU’s most-targeted country is counterattacking against fake news

item.title: Germany suspends military approval for long stays abroad for men under 45
item.description: The requirement to obtain permission had gone largely unnoticed until a German newspaper reported on it.

item.title: Nigeria begins mass trial of 500 terrorism suspects
item.description: Very few people are ever prosecuted following terror attacks with suspects often held without trial.

item.title: Lib Dems pledge extra £300m for social care at Senedd campaign launch
item.description: Leader Jane Dodds says her party can "influence the direction of the next government" after 7 May.

item.title: Cameroon 'military contractors' killed in Russia-Ukraine war - BBC confirms leaked message
item.description: The government usually refrains from commenting on the participation of its citizens in the conflict.

item.title: Dozens killed as Angola flood death toll rises
item.description: Mourning the deaths, the president says it was a "race against the clock" to find others affected.

item.title: Greece to ban social media for under-15s from next year
item.description: It follows similar moves in other European countries, including France and Spain.

item.title: British teen dies in reported Vietnam motorbike fall
item.description: Orla Wates is remembered by her mother as "beautiful, independent and very funny".

item.title: Man fined after breaking into viral hippo Moo Deng's enclosure
item.description: The director of the Thai zoo where Moo Deng lives said security would be upped after the incident.

item.title: What to Know About the U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire
item.description: The agreement to pause the fighting for two weeks came hours after President Trump threatened to destroy Iran’s “whole civilization.” Much remains uncertain.

item.title: Madagascar declares state of emergency over severe fuel shortages linked to Iran war
item.description: The presidency says it is taking action as it fears fuel shortages could lead to public disorder.

item.title: Co-founder of Jalisco New Generation drug cartel pleads guilty
item.description: Érick Valencia Salazar was among a group of 29 alleged drug lords extradited to the US from Mexico.

item.title: Hospitals coping well with doctors' strike so far - NHS boss
item.description: Resident doctors in England – the new name for junior doctors – are taking part in their 15th walkout in a long-running pay dispute.

item.title: How Pakistan helped secure a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran
item.description: Pakistan has a historic relationship with Iran, but an agreement was far from certain, writes the BBC’s Caroline Davies

item.title: Iran and America agree to pause their war
item.description: A two-week truce is a start but questions remain about a more enduring deal

item.title: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
item.description: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

item.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson released after kidnap in Iraq, officials say
item.description: US officials say they are now "working to support her safe departure from Iraq".

item.title: Plan to scrap presidential elections puts Zimbabweans at loggerheads
item.description: The ruling party has unveiled a draft law that strips voters of the chance to elect the president.

item.title: The US refinery now processing Venezuelan oil
item.description: Chevron is now importing 250,000 barrels of crude per day from Venezuela.

item.title: ‘I Love Viktor’: Trump and Vance Cheer on Orban in Hungarian Race
item.description: The American leaders slathered on the praise for the nationalist standard-bearer just days before an election he could lose.

item.title: Honduran newlywed wife of US soldier freed by ICE after detention
item.description: Annie Ramos, an undocumented immigrant who came to the US as a toddler, had gone to the base to complete paperwork so the couple could move in together.

item.title: Trump condemned over threat that Iran's 'civilisation will die'
item.description: The UN's chief says he is "deeply troubled" by such statements, as the US president ramps up pressure on Iran to agree a deal to end the war.

item.title: The crew of Artemis II are returning to a planet they have cheered up
item.description: The mission has recaptured some of the enthusiasm of early lunar flights

item.title: Kanye West ‘Should Never Have Been Invited,’ Starmer Says After Barring Rapper From UK
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s government blocked the artist formerly known as Kanye West from entering Britain for the three-day Wireless Festival in July.

item.title: Wireless Festival cancelled after Kanye West blocked from coming to UK
item.description: West was due to headline the festival in July but drew criticism over past antisemitic comments.

item.title: French couple leave Iran after more than three years in jail
item.description: Cecile Kohler and Jacques Paris are on their way home after being allowed to leave the country, the French president says.

item.title: JD Vance backs Orbán's re-election bid in Budapest visit and hits out at EU
item.description: US Vice-President JD Vance intervenes in the campaign to give Orbán a ringing endorsement in Budapest.

item.title: Fifa starts disciplinary action over Spain v Egypt chants
item.description: Fifa opens disciplinary proceedings against the Spanish FA after "Islamophobic and xenophobic" chants during a friendly between Spain and Egypt last month.

item.title: Did Israel overestimate the damage to Iran’s missile programme?
item.description: Iran shows no sign of running out

item.title: Schools in knife crime hotspots to get specialist training
item.description: A new partnership will provide training for school leaders about knife-crime risk, the government says.

item.title: Middle East Dispatch newsletter: A terrifying wait
item.description: Gregg Carlstrom, our Middle East correspondent, on Donald Trump’s blood-curdling threat to erase Iran

item.title: A wary rapprochement between India and China
item.description: New Indian investment rules are a cautious start

item.title: Pakistan Says It Hit a Military Target. Investigations Suggest It Was a Rehab Center.
item.description: After the deadliest attack in Afghanistan since the Taliban took over, families searched among photos and remains for signs of their relatives.

item.title: Scottish Conservatives pledge tax cuts in election manifesto
item.description: Leader Russell Findlay announces his party's plans ahead of next month's Holyrood poll.

item.title: One gunman killed and two injured in shooting at Israeli consulate in Istanbul
item.description: No Israeli diplomats are currently in Turkey and the Istanbul consulate has been empty for the past two-and-a-half years.

item.title: Carnival fever hits Lagos as locals celebrate Afro-Brazilian heritage
item.description: The centuries-old Fanti Carnival returned to Nigeria on Monday with a burst of colour and spectacle.

item.title: WHO suspends Gaza medical evacuations after contractor killed by Israeli troops
item.description: Israel's military says troops fired at a vehicle driven by the Palestinian because they believed it posed "an immediate threat".

item.title: Ringleader of suspected human trafficking network arrested in Ethiopia
item.description: Yetbarek Dawit is alleged to have operated a multi-million-dollar operation, using torture to raise money.

item.title: Reform would deny visas over calls for slavery reparations
item.description: The party says it wants to put African and Caribbean countries "on notice" after a vote at the UN last month.

item.title: One dead as train travelling 99mph collides with lorry in France
item.description: The lorry driver is in custody after the train driver died during the collision in northern France.

item.title: Greens promise rent controls and £1 bus fares in Welsh election manifesto
item.description: The Greens' set of promises for the Senedd election also include free bus travel for under-22s.

item.title: Chilean woman accused of Pinochet-era kidnaps loses extradition battle
item.description: Adriana Rivas moved from Chile to Australia in the 1970s, working there as a nanny and cleaner.

item.title: Plan 2 student loan interest rates capped at 6% in England
item.description: The cap on Plan 2 and postgraduate loan interest rates comes amid a risk of rising inflation.

item.title: Vietnam's leader To Lam strengthens power in unanimous assembly vote
item.description: Vietnam's communist party chief is now also the country's president - an unusual concentration of power.

item.title: More than 1,700 Brits who fell ill in Cape Verde join action against Tui
item.description: Tui is investigating the claims and says it is "not in a position to provide a statement at this stage".

item.title: Air India CEO steps down early as losses mount
item.description: Wilson, whose term was set to end in 2027, will remain CEO and MD until a successor is appointed.

item.title: Ukraine Ramps Up Attacks on Russian Oil, Aiming to Curb Iran War Windfall
item.description: As the Persian Gulf conflict boosts the oil revenue that finances Moscow’s war against Ukraine, Kyiv’s forces are striking at Russia’s ability to refine and ship its crude.

item.title: India's top court hears challenges to ruling on women's entry into temple
item.description: Experts say the court's decision will have far-reaching consequences for women's religious freedoms in India.

item.title: Minnesota deny Colombia's Rodriguez has rare muscle wasting condition
item.description: Minnesota United deny rumours that midfielder James Rodriguez is suffering from a potentially life-threatening muscle-wasting condition.

item.title: Why Real Madrid tie could make or break Kane's Ballon d'Or hopes
item.description: Harry Kane is the leading goalscorer in Europe but does he need to win a major trophy to have a chance of winning the prestigious award?

item.title: Trump's deadline looms but Asian nations already have deals with Iran
item.description: Nations in the region have been keen to reach agreements as their economies are heavily reliant on Middle East energy.

item.title: Fake Australian, Chinese and Brazilian police stations: BBC goes inside a seized scam compound
item.description: Almost nothing was known about the Royal Hill casino until the Thai military took control of it in December.

item.title: Little sign of breakthrough as Trump's Iran deadline nears
item.description: The US president is in a delicate political position as the final hours to Tuesday's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz tick down.

item.title: How rescue of US airman in remote part of Iran unfolded
item.description: The operation to extract him from the ground in hostile territory was hugely complex and involved multiple US government agencies.

item.title: Nine policemen sentenced to death in India over Covid custody killings
item.description: The father and son died in jail after being arrested on suspicion of breaching Covid lockdown rules in 2020.

item.title: Iran and America are spinning stories about the size of their victories
item.description: The bombast obscures the scale of the damage the war is doing

item.title: Seven Eritrean players fail to return home after international match
item.description: Several footballers have absconded in the past leaving behind what rights groups call a repressive government.

item.title: The strange, multicultural slang of Toronto’s teenagers
item.description: London rappers have an outsize influence

item.title: Taiwan and China are preparing for a summit, of sorts
item.description: A trip to the mainland by the KMT’s leader exposes a rift in the party

item.title: How far do the European Union’s military ambitions go?
item.description: An interview with the EU’s top military officer

item.title: Pogacar may face fine for running red light in Flanders win
item.description: Tadej Pogacar wins a record-equalling third Tour of Flanders, but could face action after he is one of up to 20 riders who run a red light at a railway crossing.

item.title: Emergency jabs after 100 children die of suspected measles in a month in Bangladesh
item.description: More than 100 people, mostly children, have been killed by measles since mid-March, officials suspect.
